title: Errors
description: An occurrence of an unhandled error captured by a Honeybadger client. Multiple notices with the same root cause are grouped under a single fault_id. Use for any query about errors, exceptions, failures, or "what's broken."

uuid.description: Stable per-notice token (use to look up a specific notice in the UI).

ulid.description: ULID for the notice — sortable, encodes the receive timestamp.

fault_id.description: Aggregated error group ID. Group by this to count distinct errors.

message.description: Error message text from the exception.

file.description: Top-frame source file from the backtrace.

hostname.description: Hostname of the server that reported the error.

revision.description: Source revision (commit SHA) the app was running at.

user.description: Resolved from the project's user-search field (typically email or user_id). Use unique(user::str) to count affected users.

request.description: HTTP request metadata from the error context.

context.description: Caller-supplied context hash. Common subkeys: context.user_email, context.user_id, context.username. Reference as context.<name>::str (or appropriate type).

session.description: Caller-supplied session hash. Reference subkeys as session.<name>::type.

params.description: HTTP params from the request. Reference subkeys as params.<name>::type.
